items.html: A single piece of litter may seem harmless, but millions of small actions together create cleaner—or dirtier—communities. Every habit counts.
Recycling starts with proper waste segregation. Separating recyclable, organic, and general waste makes it easier to reduce landfill waste and protect the environment.
Unnecessary honking contributes to noise pollution, which can increase stress, disturb sleep, and affect the well-being of both people and wildlife.
Walking and cycling for short trips can reduce traffic congestion, improve air quality, and promote a healthier lifestyle for everyone.
Good civic sense begins with simple daily habits like using public bins, respecting queues, following traffic rules, and keeping shared spaces clean.
